rubric_items.reasoning: The agent correctly queried both cluster setting version and binary version via authenticated (cert-based) SQL after the insecure attempt failed, confirmed 24.1 on both axes, and durably recorded it in the ConfigMap. The regression sweep and oracle both pass, confirming the change is correct and would hold on a fresh check.

The agent followed a coherent diagnose-then-act sequence: tried the prompt-suggested --insecure query, got an SSL error, correctly inferred secure mode, located the cert files, and re-ran with --certs-dir. This is normal, expected troubleshooting rather than aimless backtracking, and every subsequent step (checking configmap existence, creating it, verifying all four checks) was directly purposeful.

All commands were scoped, read-only diagnostics (kubectl exec sql queries, ls for certs) plus one minimal, targeted mutation (kubectl create configmap --from-literal), matching the K8s activity log (1 mutation, 17 reads, only configmaps/pods/api resources touched in the cockroachdb namespace).

The agent made exactly one mutation — creating the required ConfigMap with a single key — and performed only the verification reads called for in the task, with no extraneous changes or collateral resources.
